member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.EntrypointType
member.summary: Specifies the type of entrypoint for a Python application.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Python.EntrypointType.Executable
member.summary: A standalone executable from the virtual environment (e.g., "uvicorn", "flask", "pytest").

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Python.EntrypointType.Script
member.summary: A Python script file to execute directly (e.g., "main.py", "app.py").

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Python.EntrypointType.Module
member.summary: A Python module to run via python -m <module> (e.g., "flask", "uvicorn").

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonAppResource
member.summary: Represents a Python application resource in the distributed application model.

remarks.para: This resource allows Python applications (scripts, web servers, APIs, background services) to run as part of a distributed application. The resource manages the Python executable, working directory, and lifecycle of the Python application.

Python applications can expose HTTP endpoints, communicate with other services, and participate in service discovery like other Aspire resources. They support automatic OpenTelemetry instrumentation for observability when configured with the appropriate Python packages.

This resource supports various Python execution environments including:
System Python installations
Virtual environments (venv)
Conda environments
UV-based Python environments

member.param: The name of the resource in the application model.

The path to the Python executable. This can be:
An absolute path: "/usr/bin/python3"
A relative path: "./venv/bin/python"
A command on the PATH: "python" or "python3"
The executable is typically located in a virtual environment's bin (Linux/macOS) or Scripts (Windows) directory.

The working directory for the Python application. Python scripts and modules will be resolved relative to this directory. This is typically the root directory of your Python project containing your main script and any local modules.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonAppResource.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Represents a Python application resource in the distributed application model.

remarks.para: This resource allows Python applications (scripts, web servers, APIs, background services) to run as part of a distributed application. The resource manages the Python executable, working directory, and lifecycle of the Python application.

Python applications can expose HTTP endpoints, communicate with other services, and participate in service discovery like other Aspire resources. They support automatic OpenTelemetry instrumentation for observability when configured with the appropriate Python packages.

This resource supports various Python execution environments including:
System Python installations
Virtual environments (venv)
Conda environments
UV-based Python environments

member.param: The name of the resource in the application model.

The path to the Python executable. This can be:
An absolute path: "/usr/bin/python3"
A relative path: "./venv/bin/python"
A command on the PATH: "python" or "python3"
The executable is typically located in a virtual environment's bin (Linux/macOS) or Scripts (Windows) directory.

The working directory for the Python application. Python scripts and modules will be resolved relative to this directory. This is typically the root directory of your Python project containing your main script and any local modules.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonEntrypointAnnotation
member.summary: Stores the entrypoint configuration for a Python application resource.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonEntrypointAnnotation.Type
member.summary: Gets or sets the type of entrypoint — Executable, Script, or Module.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonEntrypointAnnotation.Entrypoint
member.summary: Gets or sets the entrypoint string (script path, module name, or executable name).

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonEnvironmentAnnotation.CreateVenvIfNotExists
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to automatically create the virtual environment if it doesn't exist. Defaults to true.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonExecutableDebuggableAnnotation
member.summary: Marks a Python resource as debuggable.

remarks.para: This annotation indicates that the Python resource supports debugging. When this annotation is present, the resource will be configured with appropriate debug launch configurations.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonInstallCommandAnnotation
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the Python package manager's install command.
member.param: The command line arguments for the Python package manager's install command. This includes the command itself (i.e. "install", "sync").

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonInstallCommandAnnotation.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the Python package manager's install command.
member.param: The command line arguments for the Python package manager's install command. This includes the command itself (i.e. "install", "sync").

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonInstallCommandAnnotation.Args
member.summary: Gets the command-line arguments supplied to the Python package manager.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonInstallerResource
member.summary: A resource that represents a Python package installer task for a Python application.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The parent Python application resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonInstallerResource.#ctor(System.String,Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonAppResource)
member.summary: A resource that represents a Python package installer task for a Python application.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The parent Python application resource.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonPackageInstallerAnnotation
member.summary: Represents an annotation for a Python installer resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonPackageInstallerAnnotation.#ctor(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ExecutableResource)
member.summary: Represents an annotation for a Python installer resource.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonPackageInstallerAnnotation.Resource
member.summary: The instance of the Installer resource used.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonPackageManagerAnnotation
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the Python package manager used in a resource.
member.param: The name of the executable used to run the package manager.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonPackageManagerAnnotation.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Represents the annotation for the Python package manager used in a resource.
member.param: The name of the executable used to run the package manager.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonPackageManagerAnnotation.ExecutableName
member.summary: Gets the executable used to run the Python package manager.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonVenvCreatorResource
member.summary: A resource that represents a Python virtual environment creator task for a Python application.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The parent Python application resource.
The path where the virtual environment should be created.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonVenvCreatorResource.#ctor(System.String,Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonAppResource,System.String)
member.summary: A resource that represents a Python virtual environment creator task for a Python application.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The parent Python application resource.
The path where the virtual environment should be created.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonVenvCreatorResource.VenvPath
member.summary: Gets the path where the virtual environment will be created.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonVersionDetector.DetectVersion(System.String,Aspire.Hosting.Python.VirtualEnvironment)
member.summary: Detects the Python version from .python-version file, pyproject.toml, or virtual environment.
member.param: The directory containing the Python application.
The virtual environment to check as a fallback.
member.returns: The detected Python version in major.minor format (e.g., "3.13"), or null if not found.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonVersionDetector.DetectVersionFromVirtualEnvironment(Aspire.Hosting.Python.VirtualEnvironment)
member.summary: Detects the Python version by executing the Python executable from the virtual environment.
member.param: The virtual environment.
member.returns: The detected Python version in major.minor format, or null if detection fails.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonVersionDetector.RequiresPythonRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
requires-python\\s*=\\s*["'](?:>=|==)?(\\d+\\.\\d+)
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase
Explanation:
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Qq].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Ii].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Ss].
○ Match '-'.
○ Match a character in the set [Pp].
○ Match a character in the set [Yy].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a character in the set [Hh].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match '='.
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match a character in the set ["'].
○ Optional (greedy).
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match the string ">=".
○ Match the string "==".
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ Match '.'.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.PythonVersionDetector.PythonVersionOutputRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
Python\\s+(\\d+)\\.(\\d+)
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase
Explanation:
○ Match a character in the set [Pp].
○ Match a character in the set [Yy].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a character in the set [Hh].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a whitespace character atomically at least once.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ Match '.'.
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.UvicornAppResource
member.summary: Represents a Uvicorn-based Python application resource that can be managed and executed within a Python environment.

remarks.para: This resource is specifically designed for Python web applications that use Uvicorn as their ASGI server, which is commonly used with frameworks like FastAPI, Starlette, and other ASGI applications.
The resource automatically configures HTTP endpoints and sets up appropriate Uvicorn-specific command-line arguments for host binding and port configuration.

member.param: The unique name used to identify the Uvicorn application resource.
The path to the Python executable. This is typically the Python executable within a virtual environment.
The working directory for the Uvicorn application. This is typically the root directory of your Python project containing your ASGI application module.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.UvicornAppResource.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Represents a Uvicorn-based Python application resource that can be managed and executed within a Python environment.

remarks.para: This resource is specifically designed for Python web applications that use Uvicorn as their ASGI server, which is commonly used with frameworks like FastAPI, Starlette, and other ASGI applications.
The resource automatically configures HTTP endpoints and sets up appropriate Uvicorn-specific command-line arguments for host binding and port configuration.

member.param: The unique name used to identify the Uvicorn application resource.
The path to the Python executable. This is typically the Python executable within a virtual environment.
The working directory for the Uvicorn application. This is typically the root directory of your Python project containing your ASGI application module.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Python.VirtualEnvironment
member.summary: Handles location of files within the virtual environment of a python app.
member.param: The path to the directory containing the python app files.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.VirtualEnvironment.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Handles location of files within the virtual environment of a python app.
member.param: The path to the directory containing the python app files.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Python.VirtualEnvironment.VirtualEnvironmentPath
member.summary: The path to the virtual environment.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Python.VirtualEnvironment.GetExecutable(System.String)
member.summary: Locates an executable in the virtual environment.
member.param: The name of the executable.
member.returns: Returns the path to the executable if it exists in the virtual environment.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for adding Python applications to an .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.AddPythonApp(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a Python application to the application model.
member.param: The to add the resource to.
The name of the resource.
The path to the directory containing the python application.
The path to the script relative to the app directory to run.

remarks.para:
This method executes a Python script directly using python script.py. By default, the virtual environment is resolved using the following priority:
If .venv exists in the app directory, use it.
If .venv exists in the AppHost directory, use it.
Otherwise, default to .venv in the app directory.
Use to specify a different virtual environment path. Use WithArgs to pass arguments to the script.
Python applications automatically have debugging support enabled.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.AddPythonModule(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a Python module to the application model.
member.param: The to add the resource to.
The name of the resource.
The path to the directory containing the python application.
The name of the Python module to run (e.g., "flask", "uvicorn").

remarks.para: This method runs a Python module using python -m <module>. By default, the virtual environment folder is expected to be named .venv and located in the app directory. Use to specify a different virtual environment path. Use WithArgs to pass arguments to the module.
Python modules automatically have debugging support enabled.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.AddPythonExecutable(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a Python executable to the application model.
member.param: The to add the resource to.
The name of the resource.
The path to the directory containing the python application.
The name of the executable in the virtual environment (e.g., "pytest", "uvicorn", "flask").

remarks.para: This method runs an executable from the virtual environment's bin directory. By default, the virtual environment folder is expected to be named .venv and located in the app directory. Use to specify a different virtual environment path. Use WithArgs to pass arguments to the executable.

Unlike scripts and modules, Python executables do not have debugging support enabled by default. Use to explicitly enable debugging support if the executable is a Python-based tool that can be debugged.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.AddPythonApp(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Adds a python application with a virtual environment to the application model.
member.param: The to add the resource to.
The name of the resource.
The path to the directory containing the python app files.
The path to the script relative to the app directory to run.
The arguments for the script.

remarks.para: This overload is obsolete. Use one of the more specific methods instead:

item.description: - To run a Python script file
- To run a Python module via python -m
- To run an executable from the virtual environment

remarks.para: Chain with WithArgs to pass arguments:

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.AddPythonApp(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Adds a python application with a virtual environment to the application model.
member.param: The to add the resource to.
The name of the resource.
The path to the directory containing the python app files.
The path to the script to run, relative to the app directory.
Path to the virtual environment.
The arguments for the script.

remarks.para: This overload is obsolete. Use one of the more specific methods instead:

item.description: - To run a Python script file
- To run a Python module via python -m
- To run an executable from the virtual environment

remarks.para: Chain with and WithArgs:

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.AddUvicornApp(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a Uvicorn-based Python application to the distributed application builder with HTTP endpoint configuration.
member.param: The distributed application builder to which the Uvicorn application resource will be added.
The unique name of the Uvicorn application resource.
The directory containing the Python application files.
The ASGI app import path which informs Uvicorn which module and variable to load as your web application. For example, "main:app" means "main.py" file and variable named "app".
member.returns: A resource builder for further configuration of the Uvicorn Python application resource.

remarks.para: This method configures the application to use Uvicorn as the ASGI server and exposes an HTTP endpoint. When publishing, it sets the entry point to use the Uvicorn executable with appropriate arguments for host and port.
By default, the virtual environment folder is expected to be named .venv and located in the app directory. Use to specify a different virtual environment path.
In non-publish mode, the --reload flag is automatically added to enable hot reload during development.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.ResolveDefaultVirtualEnvironmentPath(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves the default virtual environment path by checking multiple candidate locations.
member.param: The distributed application builder.
The Python app directory (relative to AppHost).
The relative virtual environment path (e.g., ".venv").

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.WithVirtualEnvironment``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Configures a custom virtual environment path for the Python application.
member.param: The resource builder.

The path to the virtual environment. Can be absolute or relative to the app directory. When relative, it is resolved from the working directory of the Python application. Common values include ".venv", "venv", or "myenv".
Whether to automatically create the virtual environment if it doesn't exist. Defaults to true. Set to false to disable automatic venv creation (the venv must already exist).
member.returns: A reference to the for method chaining.

remarks.para: This method updates the Python executable path to use the specified virtual environment.

By default ( = true), if the virtual environment doesn't exist, it will be automatically created before running the application (when using pip package manager, not uv). Set to false to disable this behavior and require the venv to already exist.
Virtual environments allow Python applications to have isolated dependencies separate from the system Python installation. This is the recommended approach for Python applications.

When you explicitly specify a virtual environment path using this method, the path is used verbatim. The automatic multi-location lookup (checking both app and AppHost directories) only applies when using the default ".venv" path during initial app creation via AddPythonScript, AddPythonModule, or AddPythonExecutable.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.WithDebugging``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0})
member.summary: Enables debugging support for the Python application.
member.param: The resource builder.
member.returns: A reference to the for method chaining.

remarks.para: This method adds the to the resource, which enables debugging support. The debugging configuration is automatically set up based on the entrypoint type (Script, Module, or Executable).
The debug configuration includes the Python interpreter path from the virtual environment, the program or module to debug, and appropriate launch settings.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.WithEntrypoint``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},Aspire.Hosting.Python.EntrypointType,System.String)
member.summary: Configures the entrypoint for the Python application.
member.param: The resource builder.
The type of entrypoint (Script, Module, or Executable).
The entrypoint value (script path, module name, or executable name).
member.returns: A reference to the for method chaining.

remarks.para: This method allows you to change the entrypoint configuration of a Python application after it has been created. The command and arguments will be updated based on the specified entrypoint type:

item.description: Executable: Runs the executable directly from the virtual environment

remarks.para: Important: This method resets all command-line arguments. If you need to add arguments after changing the entrypoint, call WithArgs after this method.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.WithPip``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Configures the Python resource to use pip as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.
member.typeparam: The type of the Python application resource, must derive from .
member.param: The resource builder.
When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.
The command-line arguments passed to pip install command.
member.returns: A reference to the for method chaining.

remarks.para: This method creates a child resource that runs pip install in the working directory of the Python application. The Python application will wait for this resource to complete successfully before starting.

Pip will automatically detect and use either pyproject.toml or requirements.txt based on which file exists in the application directory. If pyproject.toml exists, pip will use it. Otherwise, if requirements.txt exists, pip will use that. Calling this method will replace any previously configured package manager (such as uv).

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.PythonAppResourceBuilderExtensions.WithUv``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Adds a UV environment setup task to ensure the virtual environment exists before running the Python application.
member.typeparam: The type of the Python application resource, must derive from .
member.param: The resource builder.
When true (default), automatically runs uv sync before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.
Optional custom arguments to pass to the uv command. If not provided, defaults to ["sync"].
member.returns: A reference to the for method chaining.

remarks.para: This method creates a child resource that runs uv sync in the working directory of the Python application. The Python application will wait for this resource to complete successfully before starting.

UV (https://github.com/astral-sh/uv) is a modern Python package manager written in Rust that can manage virtual environments and dependencies with significantly faster performance than traditional tools. The uv sync command ensures that the virtual environment exists, Python is installed if needed, and all dependencies specified in pyproject.toml are installed and synchronized.
Calling this method will replace any previously configured package manager (such as pip).

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.RequiresPythonRegex_0
member.summary: Custom -derived type for the RequiresPythonRegex method.

member.summary: Scan the starting from base.runtextstart for the next match.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.RequiresPythonRegex_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.TryFindNextPossibleStartingPosition(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Search starting from base.runtextpos for the next location a match could possibly start.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.
member.returns: true if a possible match was found; false if no more matches are possible.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.RequiresPythonRegex_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.TryMatchAtCurrentPosition(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Determine whether at base.runtextpos is a match for the regular expression.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.
member.returns: true if the regular expression matches at the current position; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities
member.summary: Helper methods used by generated -derived implementations.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_defaultTimeout
member.summary: Default timeout value set in , or if none was set.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_hasTimeout
member.summary: Whether is non-infinite.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.StackPush(System.Int32[]@,System.Int32@,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Pushes 2 values onto the backtracking stack.
